title: conospermum caeruleum subsp. spathulatum
intro: Conospermum caeruleum subsp. spathulatum, also known as the Blue Pine Cones or Blue Cones Conospermum, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Proteaceae. It is native to Western Australia and has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever and treating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Conospermum caeruleum subsp. spathulatum can be prepared into herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
